item.title: There’s no cure. Just prevention. And the MMR vaccine works.
item.description:
Measles is arguably the most contagious disease on the planet. If one infected person spends an hour in a room with 100 unvaccinated individuals 90 of the will come down with the disease. And contrary to popular opinion measles is not a "benign" childhood disease - some of the victims will suffer permanent injury - blindness, brain damage, even death.
https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/books/booknews/11386603/roald-dahl-measles-warning-goes-viral.html
item.dc:creator: Xalten2

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1494 (Tylenol-American)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
Here's hoping I don't fuck up the new job.
Pippin tax Today's message from Pippin: "Good boy Good boy! Pippin!" ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin

item.title: GovBrief Today #256
item.description: ?️ Today should have been Donald Trump's triumph. He flew to Israel, was lauded at the Knesset, then went to Egypt, where he signed a Gaza accord with other mediating countries. Then he flew back home to give a posthumous Medal of Freedom tomorrow. But his victory lap was part of a split screen. At home, conservative and liberal media outlets announced their refusal to adhere to new Pentagon press restrictions. And North Carolina Republicans announced they'd gerrymander voting maps next week, something 78% of NC Republicans oppose. The night ended with his Treasury Secretary picking a fight with China that would sink Asian markets by morning. People are returning home in the Middle East tonight, which is good, but this agreement is not the Camp David Accords. There was genocide. There is famine. There are orphans by the thousands. This is where the hard work of governing happens. Trump's problem isn't that he won't govern. It's that his governing reveals he is terrible at it. That’s why you shouldn’t leave truth to the algorithm. Our free and private nightly email arms you with images, data, and quotes so you can see it all for yourself. Add your email at govbrief dot today or on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com [1] https://go.govbrief.today/mali-us-visa-bond [2] https://go.govbrief.today/us-china-dispute-bessent [3] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-zelenskyy-talks-friday [4] https://go.govbrief.today/israel-hamas-trump-declaration [5] https://go.govbrief.today/nc-gop-map-gerrymandering [6] https://go.govbrief.today/pentagon-press-policy-rebuffed [7] https://go.govbrief.today/us-tariffs-consumer-cost [8] https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-overweight-troops-illinois [9] https://go.govbrief.today/us-attorney-office-turnover [10] https://go.govbrief.today/broadview-ice-protest-area [11] https://go.govbrief.today/secure-border-act-suit [12] https://go.govbrief.today/cisa-shutdown-layoffs [13] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-fake-treasury-sponsorship [14] https://go.govbrief.today/noem-tsa-video-refusals Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Movie recommendations
item.description:
Killing Mary Sue (2025) I just watched and it had a lot of funny parts. I went in blind, and I think that's best, so I want give any spoilers. I think some of you might be turned off by it took quickly by not understanding where it's going, so I'll say, it's an intentionally absurd movie, and the power creep reaches heaven. Free on Tubi and I never see ads, so their either aren't any, or something is blocking them https://tubitv.com/movies/100044643/killing-mary-sue
Succubus (2024) I think this movie has a lot of interesting elements. There's a lot of haunting movies where you're not really sure the haunter's motives, but here, you know what the Succubus's motives are. Again, best to go in blind, so I what give more detials https://tubitv.com/movies/100039496/succubus
Tucker & Dale vs. Evil (2010) This is a classic, and a great comedy "horror" movie. If you haven't see it you should. It has the always amazing Alan Tudyk co-staring in it. Also if you like the Final Destination film series, once you've watched Tucker & Dale vs. Evil (2010) then watch this youtube video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hpPGDw328vg it's titled TUCKER & DALE VS. EVIL | The Secret Final Destination Film, and I think it makes a really good case for T&D vs. E being part of the Final Destination film series https://tubitv.com/movies/437414/tucker-dale-vs-evil
item.dc:creator: DropDrop

item.title: Weirdly, County gop disapproved this post
item.dc:creator: jackshellacdos

item.title: 20 Nice and wholesome things to brighten your day, vol 81
item.description:
Photographed by Martin Schoeller for The New Yorker in 2002: "I was hired by the New Yorker in 2002 to photograph Robin Williams, and after doing my research what stood out most for me was that he was a very physical comedian. I came up with this idea to photograph him swinging from a chandelier in a grand hotel room. Most publicists shoot down these kinds of wild ideas, so I didn’t tell anyone what I was up to, but rigged up a chandelier at the Waldorf Astoria hotel for him to swing from. When Robin got there and saw what was happening, he lifted up his shirt and showed me this enormous scar on his shoulder. He’d just had surgery and couldn’t so much as lift his arm. He was so disappointed! He really felt bad about not being able to do it, because he loved the idea and really wanted to help me accomplish my vision. Unlike most Hollywood stars, he was unfazed by his success and position. He talked to everyone from stylists to the crew, to the hotel staff. We ended up asking a maid at the hotel to swing from the chandelier instead, and I asked him to just sit there and read a newspaper, which I think in the end was an even funnier, more unexpected picture.
kitty education
item.dc:creator: hoopsnek
